ucs.title: [LLM] APT37 (ScarCruft / Reaper) RokRat & Dolphin C2 over legitimate cloud-storage APIs (pCloud / Yandex Disk / Dropbox)
ucs.description: Hunts APT37's signature C2 design — RokRat, Dolphin and Chinotto exfiltrate to and beacon from legitimate cloud-storage REST endpoints (pCloud, Yandex Disk, Dropbox, Google Drive) instead of attacker-owned infra. Flags non-browser / non-sync-client processes touching those APIs, especially LOLBins or processes living in user-writable paths.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Network_Traffic.dest_ip) as dest_ip values(Network_Traffic.dest_port) as dest_port values(Network_Traffic.url) as url values(Network_Traffic.bytes_out) as bytes_out from datamodel=Network_Traffic where (Network_Traffic.url IN ("*api.pcloud.com*","*p-eu1.pcloud.com*","*p-us1.pcloud.com*","*cloud-api.yandex.net*","*disk.yandex*","*content.dropboxapi.com*","*api.dropboxapi.com*","*www.googleapis.com/drive*","*www.googleapis.com/upload/drive*") OR Network_Traffic.dest IN ("api.pcloud.com","cloud-api.yandex.net","content.dropboxapi.com","api.dropboxapi.com")) NOT (Network_Traffic.process_name IN ("chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","brave.exe","iexplore.exe","opera.exe","OneDrive.exe","Dropbox.exe","YandexDisk*.exe","GoogleDriveFS.exe","outlook.exe","Teams.exe","slack.exe","Code.exe","Zoom.exe")) by Network_Traffic.src Network_Traffic.user Network_Traffic.process_name Network_Traffic.process_path Network_Traffic.dest | `drop_dm_object_name(Network_Traffic)` | where match(process_path, "(?i)\\\\(AppData|ProgramData|Users\\\\Public|Windows\\\\Temp|Temp)\\\\") OR match(process_name, "(?i)^(powershell|pwsh|mshta|rundll32|regsvr32|wscript|cscript|cmd|msbuild|installutil|certutil)\\.exe$") | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
let _allowed_clients = dynamic(["chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","brave.exe","iexplore.exe","opera.exe","onedrive.exe","dropbox.exe","yandexdisk.exe","yandexdisk2.exe","googledrivefs.exe","googledrivesync.exe","outlook.exe","teams.exe","msteams.exe","slack.exe","code.exe","zoom.exe","postman.exe"]);
let _lolbins = dynamic(["powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","cmd.exe","msbuild.exe","installutil.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe"]);
let _scarcruft_cloud_hosts = dynamic(["api.pcloud.com","p-eu1.pcloud.com","p-us1.pcloud.com","eapi.pcloud.com","cloud-api.yandex.net","disk.yandex.com","disk.yandex.ru","content.dropboxapi.com","api.dropboxapi.com","www.googleapis.com"]);
DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where ActionType in ("ConnectionSuccess","HttpConnectionInspected")
| where RemoteUrl has_any (_scarcruft_cloud_hosts)
| where not(RemoteUrl has_any ("googleapis.com/oauth2","googleapis.com/discovery","googleapis.com/gmail","googleapis.com/calendar"))
| where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ (_allowed_clients)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (_lolbins)
   or InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any (@"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\AppData\Roaming\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\Windows\Temp\")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName,
          InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          InitiatingProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessSHA256,
          RemoteUrl, RemoteIP, RemotePort
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let _allowed_clients = dynamic(["chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","brave.exe","iexplore.exe","opera.exe","onedrive.exe","dropbox.exe","yandexdisk.exe","googledrivefs.exe","outlook.exe","teams.exe","slack.exe","code.exe","zoom.exe"]);
let _lolbins = dynamic(["powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","cmd.exe","msbuild.exe","installutil.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe"]);
let _scarcruft_cloud_hosts = dynamic(["api.pcloud.com","p-eu1.pcloud.com","p-us1.pcloud.com","eapi.pcloud.com","cloud-api.yandex.net","disk.yandex.com","disk.yandex.ru","content.dropboxapi.com","api.dropboxapi.com","www.googleapis.com"]);
ImNetworkSession
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where Url has_any (_scarcruft_cloud_hosts) or DstHostname has_any (_scarcruft_cloud_hosts)
| where not(Url has_any ("googleapis.com/oauth2","googleapis.com/gmail","googleapis.com/calendar"))
| extend ActingProcessLeaf = tolower(tostring(split(ActingProcessName, @"\")[-1]))
| where ActingProcessLeaf !in (_allowed_clients)
| where ActingProcessLeaf in (_lolbins)
   or ActingProcessName has_any (@"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\AppData\Roaming\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\Windows\Temp\")
| project TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, SrcUsername, ActingProcessName, ActingProcessCommandLine, Url, DstIpAddr, DstPortNumber
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: APT37 RokRat / Dolphin C2 via Legitimate Cloud-Storage APIs
id: 4f6c7e0c-9b2a-4f5b-9e2c-37apt37cloudc2
status: experimental
description: Detects APT37 (ScarCruft / Reaper) RokRat & Dolphin malware beaconing to legitimate cloud-storage REST endpoints (pCloud, Yandex Disk, Dropbox, Google Drive) from non-browser / LOLBin / user-writable-path processes
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0067/
  - https://www.welivesecurity.com/2022/11/30/whos-swimming-south-korean-waters-meet-scarcrufts-dolphin/
  - https://blog.talosintelligence.com/rokrat-analysis/
author: detection-engineering
date: 2026/05/09
tags:
  - attack.command_and_control
  - attack.t1071.001
  - attack.t1102.002
  - attack.g0067
logsource:
  category: network_connection
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_host:
    DestinationHostname|contains:
      - 'api.pcloud.com'
      - 'pcloud.com'
      - 'cloud-api.yandex.net'
      - 'disk.yandex'
      - 'content.dropboxapi.com'
      - 'api.dropboxapi.com'
      - 'googleapis.com'
  selection_lolbin:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\mshta.exe'
      - '\rundll32.exe'
      - '\regsvr32.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\msbuild.exe'
      - '\certutil.exe'
      - '\bitsadmin.exe'
  selection_userpath:
    Image|contains:
      - '\AppData\Local\Temp\'
      - '\AppData\Roaming\'
      - '\ProgramData\'
      - '\Users\Public\'
      - '\Windows\Temp\'
  filter_browsers:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\chrome.exe'
      - '\msedge.exe'
      - '\firefox.exe'
      - '\OneDrive.exe'
      - '\Dropbox.exe'
      - '\GoogleDriveFS.exe'
      - '\YandexDisk.exe'
  filter_oauth:
    DestinationHostname|contains:
      - 'googleapis.com/oauth2'
      - 'googleapis.com/gmail'
      - 'googleapis.com/calendar'
  condition: selection_host and (selection_lolbin or selection_userpath) and not filter_browsers and not filter_oauth
falsepositives:
  - Custom dev tooling using cloud storage SDKs from non-default install paths
  - Internal automation scripts in PowerShell touching Drive / Dropbox
level: high
ucs.rationale: RokRat (Talos 2018-onwards), Dolphin (ESET 2022) and Chinotto (Kaspersky 2021) — APT37's signature implants — uniquely chain together pCloud + Yandex Disk + Dropbox APIs as bidirectional C2 channels rather than attacker-controlled domains. The combination of (a) those specific cloud APIs, (b) a non-vendor-sync-client requestor, and (c) execution from a LOLBin or user-writable path is the character

_sentinel_field_issues.message: `ActingProcessName` is not a column of ImNetworkSession; it does exist on ImProcessCreate, ImFileEvent, ImRegistryEvent.
`ActingProcessCommandLine` is not a column of ImNetworkSession; it does exist on ImProcessCreate, ImFileEvent, ImRegistryEvent.

_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] APT37 (Group123) weaponised LNK self-extracting PowerShell carve
ucs.description: Detects APT37's documented LNK delivery technique where the shortcut file (mailed via spear-phish targeting NK defectors / journalists / SK think-tanks) launches PowerShell which reads itself, seeks past the LNK header, decodes the embedded decoy + RokRat/Chinotto shellcode, and writes both. Flags explorer-spawned PowerShell/cmd whose command line references an .lnk file together with byte-read / base64 / substring carving primitives.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as process values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_process from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name="explorer.exe" Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","mshta.exe") (Processes.process="*.lnk*" OR Processes.process="*Get-Content*" OR Processes.process="*ReadAllBytes*" OR Processes.process="*FromBase64String*" OR Processes.process="*-Encoding Byte*" OR Processes.process="*::Copy*" OR Processes.process="*Substring(*") NOT Processes.user IN ("*$","SYSTEM","LOCAL SERVICE","NETWORK SERVICE") by host Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.parent_process_name Processes.parent_process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | where match(process, "(?i)\\.lnk") AND match(process, "(?i)(Get-Content|ReadAllBytes|FromBase64String|-Encoding\\s+Byte|Substring|::Copy|copy\\s+/b)") | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
// APT37 LNK self-carving chain — explorer-spawned shell reading an .lnk by byte
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "explorer.exe"
| where FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","mshta.exe")
| extend Cmd = tolower(ProcessCommandLine)
| where Cmd has ".lnk"
| where Cmd has_any (
    "get-content",
    "gc ",
    "readallbytes",
    "frombase64string",
    "-encoding byte",
    "-encodedcommand",
    "::copy",
    "copy /b",
    "substring(",
    "[io.file]",
    "createdecryptor",
    "writeallbytes")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          FileName, ProcessCommandLine,
          InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessParentFileName,
          InitiatingProcessAccountName, SHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// APT37 LNK self-carving chain on Sentinel ASIM ProcessCreate
ImProcessCreate
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where ActorUsername !endswith "$"
| where ParentProcessName has "explorer.exe"
| extend ChildLeaf = tolower(tostring(split(TargetProcessName, @"\")[-1]))
| where ChildLeaf in ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","mshta.exe")
| extend Cmd = tolower(TargetProcessCommandLine)
| where Cmd has ".lnk"
| where Cmd has_any ("get-content","gc ","readallbytes","frombase64string","-encoding byte","-encodedcommand","::copy","copy /b","substring(","[io.file]","createdecryptor","writeallbytes")
| project TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ActorUsername, TargetProcessName, TargetProcessCommandLine, ParentProcessName, SHA256
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: APT37 (ScarCruft / Group123) LNK Self-Extracting PowerShell Carve
id: 9d2bf3e1-1c4a-46e8-9fa5-37apt37lnkcarve
status: experimental
description: Detects APT37's signature LNK delivery where explorer launches PowerShell/cmd that reads the shortcut by byte and decodes the embedded decoy + RokRat / Chinotto shellcode. Documented in Cisco Talos Group123, ASEC, and Genians reporting on ScarCruft (TEMP.Reaper, InkySquid).
references:
  - https://blog.talosintelligence.com/who-wasnt-responsible-for-olympic/
  - https://asec.ahnlab.com/en/category/malware-information/apt-malware/scarcruft/
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0067/
author: detection-engineering
date: 2026/05/09
tags:
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.t1204.002
  - attack.t1027
  - attack.g0067
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\explorer.exe'
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\mshta.exe'
  selection_lnk:
    CommandLine|contains: '.lnk'
  selection_carve:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'Get-Content'
      - 'gc '
      - 'ReadAllBytes'
      - 'FromBase64String'
      - '-Encoding Byte'
      - '-EncodedCommand'
      - '::Copy'
      - 'copy /b'
      - 'Substring('
      - '[IO.File]'
      - 'WriteAllBytes'
      - 'CreateDecryptor'
  filter_machine:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: selection_parent and selection_child and selection_lnk and selection_carve and not filter_machine
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate admin scripts that read LNK targets via PowerShell (rare in user-mode)
  - Endpoint-management tooling enumerating Recent Items
level: high
ucs.rationale: The self-reading LNK → PowerShell byte-carve chain is the documented ScarCruft / Group123 / InkySquid initial-access pattern across Talos (2018), AhnLab ASEC (2021-2024), Genians, and ESET Dolphin (2022). The triple of (explorer parent) + (script-host child) + (.lnk literal in cmdline alongside byte-level read primitives) is highly specific to APT37 LNK staging — generic PowerShell launches lack t
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID
